extraction.one_line_summary: MARTIN Dan soloed to victory from the break with 10km remaining on the Sega di Ala final climb while BERNAL Egan cracked for the first time, losing 1:23 to YATES Simon.
tactical_patterns: MARTIN Dan attacked breakaway companions with 10km left on final climb, timing move after stage shortening crash descent.
YATES Simon exploited BERNAL's first weakness on final climb, gaining 53 seconds on GC despite 3+ minute deficit entering stage.
ALMEIDA João closed 13 seconds on MARTIN despite starting climb from peloton, revealing stronger position than stage result suggests.
MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe domestique work failed to rescue cracking BERNAL on final climb, rare Ineos mountain stage vulnerability exposed.

rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Won from 19-rider break by dropping final companions 10km out, stage racing timing superior to pure climbers.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: First GC crack lost 1:23 to YATES, bad back mentioned, MARTÍNEZ unable to pace him back on final climb.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Gained 53 seconds on BERNAL while finishing 30 seconds down on stage, reopened GC battle from 3:23 deficit.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Finished second only 13 seconds behind solo winner despite starting final climb in peloton, climbed back into top 10 overall.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: Dropped badly on final climb, fell two GC places to sixth at 6:09, losing contact before main GC action.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: Distanced on final climb, dropped from GC contention to 6:03 back in fourth position.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Very bad day mentioned specifically, lost significant time though exact gap not detailed in commentary.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: Stayed with BERNAL during crisis, maintained second overall at 2:21, demonstrated superior climbing form to leader.
rider_signals.BOUCHARD Geoffrey: positive: Survived both major climbs in front group, present in final six riders before MARTIN's decisive move.
rider_signals.PEDRERO Antonio: positive: One of four survivors over Paso San Valentino, maintained front group position until late-stage selection.
